title: watch.rs races between symlink check and file read
description: In watch.rs watch_loop(), the symlink check calls tokio::fs::symlink_metadata() and then 200ms later calls tokio::fs::read_to_string() via process_file(). An attacker who can write to the watched directory can replace a regular file with a symlink in that 200ms window. The result is that an arbitrary file readable by the broadside process (e.g., /etc/passwd, the private key DB) gets published as a post to all followers.
close_reason: Fixed: watch.rs now canonicalizes path after read and rejects escapes.
